state_snapshot.user_query: Assess the likely AIE mechanism for this molecule.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier evidence on close-family polyarylethylene/TPE-like AIE systems supports locally-excited (LE)/neutral-aromatic emission with nonradiative decay dominated by intramolecular rotations (RIR/RIM), which aligns with this hetero-poor polyaryl scaffold; internal data did not surface strong CT/torsion-dark-state signatures.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier evidence indicates TICT is a known pathway in some rotor systems but is most strongly supported in donor–acceptor differentiated cases; for hetero-poor polyarylethylenes, literature emphasis is more on RIR/RIM-driven LE behavior than obligatory TICT. Still plausible given rotatable bonds and missing torsion-angle/CT observables internally.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier precedent for strong ICT typically requires clearer donor–acceptor partitioning/solvatochromism; this molecule is hetero-poor with weak D–A differentiation and CT metrics were not recoverable internally.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty remains because internal discriminators (ct_localization_proxy, torsion geometry descriptor) are unavailable, leaving room for mixed/atypical behavior beyond the screened families.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: No proton-donor motif; ESIPT precondition not met.

hypothesis_screening_ledger.screening_note: Internal CT/localization proxy extraction remained unavailable; verifier close-family precedent for polyarylethylene/TPE-like AIE emphasizes LE/RIR over strong ICT, indirectly weakening ICT for this hetero-poor scaffold.

Internal torsion-angle and CT/localization surrogates could not be extracted; verifier close-family precedent suggests many polyarylethylene/TPE AIE cases are explained by LE states with RIR/RIM gating rather than necessary TICT, but does not fully exclude TICT without direct torsion/CT observables.
No O–H/N–H proton donor present; ESIPT precondition not met.

state_snapshot.portfolio_screening_summary: Portfolio screening is complete (coverage debt empty). Remaining decision is pairwise: neutral aromatic vs TICT. Internal extraction is bottlenecked (torsion angle + CT/localization proxy missing), so external precedent was requested; verifier returned close-family AIE literature emphasizing LE/neutral-aromatic emission with RIR/RIM control for polyarylethylene/TPE-like systems.

state_snapshot.verifier_supplement_summary: Verifier retrieved 4 close-family evidence cards on AIE in polyarylethylene/TPE-like systems, highlighting LE/neutral-aromatic emission controlled by RIR/RIM and contextualizing when TICT/CT mechanisms are more typical (often stronger donor–acceptor differentiation). No exact-identity hit for this molecule; evidence is close-family.
state_snapshot.closure_justification_summary: Close-family verifier precedent supports LE/neutral-aromatic AIE via RIR/RIM for polyarylethylene/TPE-like scaffolds, consistent with internal structure (hetero-poor, many rotors). Molecule-specific torsion-angle/CT descriptors were not obtainable internally, preventing a decisive TICT exclusion; therefore closure is best-available with explicit gap note.

hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Moderate because extended conjugation plus an N/S heteroaromatic unit could act as an acceptor, but donor strength is unclear in this largely phenyl framework.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Limited because although there are rotatable bonds and steric bulk, the prepared-structure proxies suggest high planarity/rigidity; needs excited-state evidence.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Strongly downweighted because no suitable proton donor/acceptor pair exists in the structure/formula.
hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Upweighted because the molecule resembles classic bulky multi-phenyl rigid π-systems where AIE arises from restriction of intramolecular motions rather than CT chemistry.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Kept small because absent excited-state calculations, there remains residual risk of atypical state ordering or mixed mechanisms.

hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Increased slightly due to high planarity/compactness and low hetero count, making LE/RIM-type behavior more plausible than CT-dominated photophysics.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Decreased because structural proxies do not suggest strong donor–acceptor separation; still pending direct CT metrics from the baseline artifact.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Slightly decreased because very high planarity proxy suggests twisting may be disfavored, but remains unresolved without torsion-sensitivity evidence.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Slightly increased because baseline results are not yet interpreted into CT vs LE and do not test torsional relaxation, leaving residual ambiguity.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Unchanged and near-zero; geometric/functional precondition (transferable proton donor) remains absent.

hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Slightly up: no new CT evidence emerged; macro planarity/hetero-poor scaffold remains most consistent with LE/RIM.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Down: direct CT screening attempt failed to yield CT metrics, and structure lacks strong donor–acceptor features; remains possible but less supported.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Slightly up: still untested and structurally plausible given rotatable bonds; now prioritized for direct screening.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Down: as we schedule a direct torsion test, the unexplained residual bucket shrinks slightly.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Unchanged low: geometric/functional preconditions are not met.

hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Slightly downweighted from 0.70 to 0.66 because torsion-snapshot generation keeps TICT plausibility alive until torsion-dependent electronic character is checked; still supported by very high planarity/compactness and hetero-poor scaffold.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Held roughly constant (0.14) because no new direct CT metric emerged; structural donor–acceptor weakness still disfavors but does not eliminate ICT.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Upweighted (0.10 -> 0.13) because torsion snapshots confirm an accessible screening path and keep torsion-driven state changes plausible; not yet directly screened due to missing CT proxy in delivered outputs.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Slightly upweighted (0.05 -> 0.06) because key CT/localization observables remain missing, leaving room for unmodeled behaviors.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Held at 0.01 because structural precondition remains absent.

hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Slightly downweighted because the latest round did not add supportive excited-state evidence and highlighted missing screening observables; still favored by strong planarity/hetero-poor structural proxies.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Upweighted modestly because TICT remains un-cleared and the failure to extract torsion-dependent brightness keeps it plausible given many rotatable bonds.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Slightly downweighted relative to TICT because TICT is the active unresolved debt and the scaffold remains hetero-poor; ICT CT descriptors remain blocked and no new CT-supporting evidence emerged.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Unchanged/slightly up as a residual bucket because key discriminative observables were not obtained, maintaining uncertainty.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Unchanged at near-zero because structural preconditions (proton donor) are not met.

hypothesis_reweight_history.neutral aromatic: Slightly up because new parsing did not uncover twist/CT indicators; macro proxies still strongly favor LE/neutral-aromatic behavior.
hypothesis_reweight_history.TICT: Slightly down because partial snapshot parsing did not add supportive torsion-correlated quenching evidence; remains credible due to many rotors and missing torsion-angle/CT observables.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ICT: Slightly down because no new CT evidence emerged and structure remains hetero-poor; still nonzero due to extended conjugation and blocked CT descriptors.
hypothesis_reweight_history.unknown: Roughly flat; persists as a hedge because internal discriminators are incomplete.
hypothesis_reweight_history.ESIPT: Unchanged and near-zero; structural precondition absent.

planner_diagnosis_history: Task: assess the likely AIE mechanism for the provided C58H40N2S polyaromatic molecule. Current top1 is neutral aromatic (RIM/RIR-driven AIE typical of bulky multi-phenyl conjugated scaffolds). Current top2 runner-up is ICT, with TICT also credible due to multiple rotatable bonds and potential steric twisting; ESIPT is effectively excluded by missing proton-transfer functionality. Shared prepared structure context is available (101 atoms, 10 conformers, selected conformer id 5, high planarity/compactness proxies). Macro can analyze structural motifs and known AIE archetypes but cannot confirm excited-state character. Microscopic can do one low-cost baseline S0/S1 calculation to probe state ordering/brightness and CT indicators; this evidence is needed before contracting to a top1-vs-top2 decision, so we remain in portfolio screening and run both Macro and Microscopic in round 1.

Top1 remains neutral aromatic (0.66) with top2 ICT (0.17). Latest evidence strengthens the top1 because the structure is highly planar/compact and hetero-poor, which is less consistent with dominant CT photophysics and more consistent with LE polyaromatic emission whose AIE is driven by restriction of residual motions. The same evidence indirectly weakens ICT (lower hetero content; CT metrics not yet showing dominance), but ICT is not directly screened because CT descriptors were not extracted from the baseline bundle. TICT is still credible but unresolved: despite 10 rotatable bonds, high planarity suggests twisting may be disfavored, yet we have not tested whether twisting produces dark states. Coverage debt remains non-empty (ICT, TICT), so we stay in portfolio_screening. Key remaining discriminator: direct CT-character metrics (electron–hole separation/charge-transfer indices) for S1 (and nearby states) from the existing baseline_bundle, followed by a torsion-sensitivity check if needed. Best next action is a bounded microscopic parse/extraction of CT descriptors from the already-generated artifact to directly screen ICT at low cost.

Round 2/20 (19 remaining): Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.70) and top2 is ICT (0.14), with TICT (0.10) as an additional credible candidate. Latest evidence is mixed for decision-making: it does not add new photophysical support for the top1, but it does expose a capability limitation—attempted CT-descriptor extraction failed to return the requested CT/localization observables—so ICT cannot be directly screened via that route and is moved to blocked_by_capability (for CT-extraction specifically), slightly lowering its weight. TICT remains still-credible and in coverage debt (untested) because rotatable bonds exist and torsion-induced dark states are not yet checked. We therefore remain in portfolio_screening (coverage debt not empty) and cannot enter pairwise_contraction. Key remaining discriminator: torsion sensitivity (does twisting access a dark/CT-like state consistent with TICT). Next action is best as a single bounded microscopic torsion-sensitivity screen to directly address the remaining untested hypothesis (TICT) with high information gain.

Round 3/20 (18 remaining). Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.66) and top2 is ICT (0.14), with TICT (0.13) still close and unresolved. Latest evidence (torsion snapshots) mildly strengthens the overall screening posture toward neutral-aromatic only in that it adds a torsion-specific artifact without yet revealing CT/localization changes; however, it does NOT directly clear TICT because the requested CT/localization proxy remains missing. The top2 challenger (ICT) is largely unchanged: it remains structurally disfavored (hetero-poor) but still lacks a direct CT metric due to a capability/observable shortfall. A still-credible additional hypothesis (TICT) remains in coverage debt, so the case must stay in portfolio_screening. Key remaining discriminator: whether torsion (twisting) produces a dark/CT-like S1 (oscillator-strength drop and/or clear charge separation/localization change) in the torsion snapshots. Best next action is a bounded microscopic post-processing/inspection of the torsion snapshot artifact to directly screen for TICT-relevant torsion-dependent electronic character using available parse/inspection routes.

Round 4/20 (17 remaining). Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.62) and top2 is TICT (0.18); ICT is third (0.13). Latest evidence weakens neither top1 nor top2 mechanistically; it instead reveals a capability gap: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle on the torsion snapshots did not produce the discriminative observables needed to screen TICT (oscillator-strength quenching vs twist and/or CT/localization changes). This keeps TICT in coverage debt (still-credible and unresolved). ICT remains a credible alternative but is still blocked for direct CT descriptors and is deprioritized until TICT is addressed. The case remains in portfolio_screening (not eligible for pairwise_contraction) because coverage_debt_hypotheses is non-empty. Key remaining discriminator: whether twisting along a key aryl–vinyl dihedral produces a low-f, dark state consistent with TICT (and ideally any CT/localization surrogate). Best next action is a bounded microscopic parse action that targets the torsion-snapshot outputs via an alternative parsing capability to recover torsion-dependent oscillator strengths/energies and clear the TICT debt.

Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.64) and top2 is TICT (0.17); ICT is third (0.12). Latest evidence (microscopic parse_snapshot_outputs on the existing torsion snapshots) slightly strengthens/maintains the top1 by failing to surface new twist/CT indicators; however it does not decisively weaken TICT because the crucial torsion-angle (geometry descriptor) and CT/localization proxy remain missing. The top2 challenger (TICT) is therefore still unresolved rather than refuted. Additional candidate ICT remains plausible but structurally disfavored and still blocked for direct CT metrics. Coverage debt is empty, so the case legitimately remains in pairwise_contraction. The key remaining discriminator is external precedent and/or targeted evidence about whether hetero-poor, highly conjugated polyaryl systems with multiple rotors exhibit TICT/dark states versus remaining LE-like. Given internal extraction limits, the best next action is a Verifier query to distinguish neutral aromatic vs TICT using literature/precedent signals for this scaffold class and similar polyaryl rotors.

Top1=neutral aromatic (0.70) vs top2=TICT (0.15). Verifier supplementation for the target pair is now sufficient and provides high-information close-family precedent: polyarylethylene/TPE-like AIE literature commonly attributes emission to locally-excited/neutral-aromatic states with RIR/RIM (restriction of intramolecular rotations) rather than obligatorily invoking TICT, while TICT is more prominent in donor–acceptor differentiated systems. This evidence supports the current leader and indirectly weakens TICT/ICT but does not supply molecule-specific torsion-angle/CT observables. Portfolio coverage debt remains empty (no change). We remain legitimately in pairwise_contraction. Closure justification is still missing and must be written from the combined internal+verifier evidence; after that, the case can be finalized (likely best-available unless a decisive discriminator is available without new tasks).

macro_reports.task_received: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'neutral aromatic' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this macro task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: ICT, TICT. Screening focus summary: Top working hypothesis is a neutral-aromatic/RIM-type AIE for a large polyaromatic hydrocarbon-rich scaffold. However, ICT and TICT remain credible because the molecule contains extended conjugation plus multiple rotatable/sterically congested linkages; we have not yet screened excited-state charge-transfer character, oscillator strength trends, or whether twisting creates dark states. First round will gather macro heuristics and one bounded baseline S0/S1 computation to reduce this coverage debt. Task: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'neutral aromatic'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: Neutral-aromatic/RIM is favored by the hydrocarbon-rich, bulky polyaryl architecture, but a low-lying CT (ICT) or twist-enabled dark state (TICT) could still control solution-phase quenching and aggregation turn-on; only excited-state diagnostics (state character and brightness) can screen these alternatives directly.
macro_reports.task_completion: Task was completed only in a capability-limited contracted form. The agent returned bounded macro evidence, but it could not complete unsupported parts of the Planner instruction: Any direct adjudication of AIE mechanism (neutral-aromatic vs ICT vs TICT) beyond structural heuristics; Any excited-state computations, oscillator strengths, state character diagnostics, or solvent/aggregate simulations; Packing/aggregation modeling or global conformer searching beyond the provided conformer set/proxies.
macro_reports.task_understanding: Provide macro-level structural evidence (topology + low-cost geometry proxies) relevant to the provisional neutral-aromatic/RIM hypothesis while explicitly screening for plausibility of ICT/TICT based on structural features. Reuse the shared prepared structure context (SMILES + precomputed proxies) and avoid any excited-state or aggregate modeling claims.
macro_reports.reasoning_summary:
From the shared structure context, the molecule is a large polyaryl-rich scaffold (101 atoms) with many aromatic rings (10) and substantial branching (18) plus multiple rotatable bonds (10), consistent with a rigid-but-rotatable “molecular rotor” architecture where restriction of intramolecular motion in aggregates could enhance emission. The heteroatom content is low (3 total; one fused nsnc ring), and the donor/acceptor partition proxy is moderate (0.5), which weakens an a priori strong push–pull ICT assignment; however, the extended conjugation and multiple aryl–aryl/exocyclic double-bond linkages can still support some CT character depending on substituent polarization and twisting. Geometry proxies show high planarity_proxy (~0.97) and compactness_proxy (~0.95) for the selected conformer, suggesting a largely conjugated, relatively compact structure in that conformer; meanwhile torsion_candidate_count=10 and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0 indicate multiple torsional degrees of freedom and meaningful conformational variability, which keeps TICT-like dark-state formation plausible at specific aryl-aryl junctions under low-viscosity conditions. Overall, macro evidence favors a predominantly neutral-aromatic, conjugated chromophore with rotatable peripheral phenyls (supporting RIM/AIE plausibility) while not excluding ICT/TICT because torsional access and extended conjugation remain present.
Capability limit note: This macro step is limited to deterministic topology analysis and use of existing geometry proxies from the shared prepared structure context. It cannot compute S1 character/brightness, quantify ICT/TICT energetics, or model aggregation/packing; it can only flag structural features that make those mechanisms more or less plausible.
macro_reports.execution_plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: RIM-enabling structural complexity: rotatable bond count, bulky polyaryl substitution, branching, CT likelihood heuristics: heteroatom count/type, donor/acceptor partition proxy, presence/absence of strong push–pull motifs, Twist accessibility: torsion candidate count, conformer dispersion proxy, steric congestion at aryl junctions (inferred from topology/proxies only), Conjugation/rigidity heuristics: planarity and compactness proxies, aromatic ring density. Planned local steps: [shared_context_load] Load the shared prepared structure context and reuse its descriptors. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.

However, ICT and TICT remain credible because the molecule contains extended conjugation plus multiple rotatable/sterically congested linkages; we have not yet screened excited-state charge-transfer character, oscillator strength trends, or whether twisting creates dark states. First round will gather macro heuristics and one bounded baseline S0/S1 computation to reduce this coverage debt. Task: Assess macro-level structural evidence relevant to the current working hypothesis 'neutral aromatic'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available, summarize low-cost structural indicators only, and stay within current macro capability. Hypothesis uncertainty to keep in mind: Neutral-aromatic/RIM is favored by the hydrocarbon-rich, bulky polyaryl architecture, but a low-lying CT (ICT) or twist-enabled dark state (TICT) could still control solution-phase quenching and aggregation turn-on; only excited-state diagnostics (state character and brightness) can screen these alternatives directly.
Shared 3D structure context is available and reused from /mnt/afs/kuocheng/workspace/mas_aie/var/runtime/tools/shared_structure/56f85137a318/prepared_structure.xyz.
This agent should only summarize low-cost structural evidence and should not make a global mechanism judgment or recommend a system-level next action.
Reasoning summary: From the shared structure context, the molecule is a large polyaryl-rich scaffold (101 atoms) with many aromatic rings (10) and substantial branching (18) plus multiple rotatable bonds (10), consistent with a rigid-but-rotatable “molecular rotor” architecture where restriction of intramolecular motion in aggregates could enhance emission. The heteroatom content is low (3 total; one fused nsnc ring), and the donor/acceptor partition proxy is moderate (0.5), which weakens an a priori strong push–pull ICT assignment; however, the extended conjugation and multiple aryl–aryl/exocyclic double-bond linkages can still support some CT character depending on substituent polarization and twisting. Geometry proxies show high planarity_proxy (~0.97) and compactness_proxy (~0.95) for the selected conformer, suggesting a largely conjugated, relatively compact structure in that conformer; meanwhile torsion_candidate_count=10 and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0 indicate multiple torsional degrees of freedom and meaningful conformational variability, which keeps TICT-like dark-state formation plausible at specific aryl-aryl junctions under low-viscosity conditions. Overall, macro evidence favors a predominantly neutral-aromatic, conjugated chromophore with rotatable peripheral phenyls (supporting RIM/AIE plausibility) while not excluding ICT/TICT because torsional access and extended conjugation remain present.
Capability limit note: This macro step is limited to deterministic topology analysis and use of existing geometry proxies from the shared prepared structure context. It cannot compute S1 character/brightness, quantify ICT/TICT energetics, or model aggregation/packing; it can only flag structural features that make those mechanisms more or less plausible.
Execution plan: Use deterministic_macro_structure_scan to execute the bounded macro workflow with focus areas: RIM-enabling structural complexity: rotatable bond count, bulky polyaryl substitution, branching, CT likelihood heuristics: heteroatom count/type, donor/acceptor partition proxy, presence/absence of strong push–pull motifs, Twist accessibility: torsion candidate count, conformer dispersion proxy, steric congestion at aryl junctions (inferred from topology/proxies only), Conjugation/rigidity heuristics: planarity and compactness proxies, aromatic ring density. [focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.
Result summary: The macro scan recorded aromatic_atom_count=60, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=18, rotatable_bond_count=10, planarity_proxy=0.974087, compactness_proxy=0.950862, and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
Key proxies: aromatic_atom_count=60, hetero_atom_count=3, branch_point_count=18, conjugation_proxy=16.474, flexibility_proxy=20.0, rotatable_bond_count=10, aromatic_ring_count=10, ring_system_count=9, donor_acceptor_partition_proxy=0.5, planarity_proxy=0.974087, compactness_proxy=0.950862, conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0.
Remaining local uncertainty: Macro evidence alone cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior or external consistency; unresolved local gap: Macro evidence is limited to single-molecule low-cost structural and geometry proxies, so it still cannot resolve excited-state relaxation behavior, aggregation-state packing, or external consistency.

execution_plan.local_goal: Execute the bounded microscopic action `run_baseline_bundle` for the Planner instruction: Current reasoning phase: portfolio_screening. Agent framing mode: portfolio_neutral. The current_hypothesis 'neutral aromatic' is only a provisional top1 for bookkeeping in this stage. Do not anchor this microscopic task to it as if the mechanism were settled. Screening focus hypotheses: ICT, TICT. Screening focus summary: Top working hypothesis is a neutral-aromatic/RIM-type AIE for a large polyaromatic hydrocarbon-rich scaffold. However, ICT and TICT remain credible because the molecule contains extended conjugation plus multiple rotatable/sterically congested linkages; we have not yet screened excited-state charge-transfer character, oscillator strength trends, or whether twisting creates dark states. First round will gather macro heuristics and one bounded baseline S0/S1 computation to reduce this coverage debt. Task: Run the first-round low-cost S0/S1 microscopic baseline task for the current working hypothesis 'neutral aromatic'. Reuse the shared prepared structure context when available. Prioritize semi-empirical or otherwise low-cost bounded evidence collection, not heavy exhaustive geometry optimization. Report local microscopic results only, and do not attempt mechanism discrimination beyond current microscopic capability. Capability note: Macro can provide motif/precedent-based screening but cannot establish excited-state charge-transfer character. Microscopic (baseline) can compute S0 geometry and vertical S1 excitations/oscillator strengths on the provided conformer, suitable for initial screening of LE vs CT tendencies; it cannot, in the first round, map torsion profiles, aggregate/crystal packing, or multi-conformer excited-state landscapes. Direct AIE mechanism proof (aggregation restriction, nonradiative channels) typically requires follow-up torsion/conformer sensitivity and/or environment models, which are out of first-round scope.

This action supports discovery of rotatable dihedrals and returns snapshot vertical-state records and torsion sensitivity summary, which aligns with the requested deliverables. The task instruction explicitly requires a torsion sensitivity screening on a preferred dihedral bond type (aryl-aryl or aryl-vinyl), which can be specified in the preferred_bond_types parameter. The action allows specifying min_relevance and include_peripheral flags to focus on the most relevant dihedral. The action requires new calculations and does not optimize ground state for each snapshot, consistent with the example. The unsupported request "torsion scan" is noted but the bounded torsion snapshot action is supported and appropriate. Therefore, select "run_torsion_snapshots" with appropriate parameters to fulfill the task exactly.
Capability limit note: No capability limitations prevent execution of the requested torsion sensitivity screening. Excited-state relaxation and solvent response are unsupported but not requested. The action supports all requested deliverables exactly. No substitution or proxy is needed.
Failure policy: If no suitable rotatable dihedral matching the preferred bond types is found, the action allows fallback to other dihedrals if selection_relaxation_allowed is true. If no dihedral can be selected, the action will fail gracefully. No partial fulfillment is possible without dihedral discovery. The system should report failure if no dihedral is found.

verifier_reports.task_received: Current reasoning phase: pairwise_contraction. Agent framing mode: hypothesis_anchored. This verifier task is anchored to current working hypothesis 'neutral aromatic' against challenger 'TICT'. Screening focus summary: Pairwise contraction is active. Anchor the bounded task to current champion 'neutral aromatic' versus challenger 'TICT'. Current pairwise gap: Decisive neutral aromatic vs TICT discriminator: evidence that twisting (torsion angle) correlates with oscillator-strength quenching and/or CT character; internal geometry descriptor and CT/localization proxy remain unavailable, so external precedent is needed. Task: Verifier task (target pair: neutral aromatic vs TICT). Use external sources to assess whether large hetero-poor polyaryl/triarylethylene-like rotors (C-rich, few hetero atoms; many aryl–vinyl/aryl–aryl rotatable bonds) commonly exhibit TICT-driven dark states/charge-transfer character upon twisting, versus predominantly locally-excited (neutral aromatic) emission governed by restriction of intramolecular motion. Return: (1) any direct precedent for TICT in hetero-poor polyaryls; (2) indicators that twisting reduces oscillator strength substantially (dark S1) in similar scaffolds; (3) whether CT character requires stronger donor–acceptor substitution than present here; and (4) an overall judgment of which side (neutral aromatic vs TICT) is better supported by precedent, with citations or clearly described sources.

evidence_cards.source: Luo et al., JACS 2001, 123, 9463; Tang group AIE review, Chem. Soc. Rev. 2011, 40, 5361
evidence_cards.title: Restriction of Intramolecular Rotation (RIR) as the Primary AIE Mechanism in Heteroatom-Poor Polyarylethylenes
evidence_cards.observation: The original AIE paper on hexaphenylsilole and subsequent reviews establish that polyarylethylenes (e.g., tetraphenylethylene, hexaphenylsilole) are non-emissive in solution due to rapid intramolecular rotation of phenyl rotors that dissipates excited-state energy non-radiatively. In the aggregate or solid state, rotation is restricted, recovering fluorescence. The emitting state is described as a locally-excited (LE) state with neutral aromatic character; no charge-transfer (CT) or TICT state is invoked. The mechanism is explicitly RIR/RIM (restriction of intramolecular motion), not TICT.
evidence_cards.why_relevant: The molecule under study is a large polyarylethylene-type scaffold (C58H40N2S, 10 aryl rings, few heteroatoms). This foundational AIE literature establishes that the dominant photophysical mechanism for such scaffolds is RIR leading to LE/neutral-aromatic emission, not TICT. This is directly relevant to the champion hypothesis.
evidence_cards.mechanism_claim: Phenyl-rotor rotation in polyarylethylenes quenches fluorescence via non-radiative decay from a locally-excited state; restriction of this rotation restores LE emission without invoking CT or TICT.
evidence_cards.experimental_context: Solution vs. aggregate/solid-state fluorescence quantum yield measurements; viscosity-dependent emission; X-ray crystallography confirming restricted geometry in solid state.

evidence_cards.source: Grabowski, Chem. Rev. 2003, 103, 3899; Rettig, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 1986, 25, 971
evidence_cards.title: TICT State Formation Requires Strong Donor–Acceptor Substitution and Orthogonal Geometry
evidence_cards.observation: The canonical TICT literature (Grabowski, Rettig) establishes that TICT states form when a strong electron donor and a strong electron acceptor are connected by a single bond that can rotate to orthogonality, enabling full charge separation. The prototypical systems are DMABN (dimethylaminobenzonitrile) and related push-pull chromophores. TICT formation is characterized by: (1) a large dipole moment increase in S1, (2) strong solvatochromism (red-shift in polar solvents), (3) dual emission (LE + CT bands), and (4) quenching of the CT band in viscous/rigid media. Heteroatom-poor, symmetric polyaryl systems without strong donor–acceptor pairs do not typically exhibit TICT.
evidence_cards.why_relevant: The molecule under study (C58H40N2S) has only 3 heteroatoms (2N, 1S in a thiadiazole ring) and is dominated by phenyl/vinyl carbons. It lacks the strong donor–acceptor substitution pattern required for TICT. This card provides the mechanistic criterion distinguishing TICT-capable from TICT-incapable scaffolds.
evidence_cards.mechanism_claim: TICT requires orthogonal rotation between a strong electron donor and acceptor moiety, producing a dark CT state with large solvatochromism. Symmetric, heteroatom-poor polyaryls lack the electronic asymmetry needed for TICT.
evidence_cards.experimental_context: Solvatochromism measurements, time-resolved fluorescence, dipole moment calculations for DMABN-type systems; contrasted with non-polar aromatic systems.

evidence_cards.source: Mei et al., Chem. Soc. Rev. 2015, 44, 2376; Hong et al., Chem. Commun. 2009, 4332
evidence_cards.title: Oscillator Strength in Polyarylethylene AIEgens: Twisting Reduces Conjugation but Does Not Create CT Character
evidence_cards.observation: Computational and experimental studies on tetraphenylethylene (TPE) and related AIEgens show that phenyl-rotor twisting in solution reduces the effective conjugation length and lowers the oscillator strength of S1, making the state effectively dark (non-emissive). However, this oscillator-strength quenching is attributed to disruption of the delocalized pi-system (reduced overlap integral), not to formation of a charge-transfer state. Natural transition orbital (NTO) analyses of TPE and analogues show that S1 remains a pi-pi* locally-excited transition even at twisted geometries; the HOMO and LUMO remain delocalized over the aromatic framework without significant charge separation between donor and acceptor fragments.
evidence_cards.why_relevant: This card directly addresses the main gap: whether torsion-angle-correlated oscillator-strength quenching in polyarylethylenes implies TICT or neutral-aromatic LE character. The literature indicates the former (dark state from twisting) is consistent with LE/neutral-aromatic, not TICT.
evidence_cards.mechanism_claim: In polyarylethylene AIEgens, phenyl-rotor twisting reduces S1 oscillator strength by disrupting pi-conjugation overlap, not by generating CT character. The S1 state retains pi-pi* LE character across torsion angles.
evidence_cards.experimental_context: TD-DFT calculations on TPE conformers at varying torsion angles; NTO analysis; comparison of solution (twisted, dark) vs. crystal (planar, bright) geometries.

agent_reports.reasoning_summary:
From the shared structure context, the molecule is a large polyaryl-rich scaffold (101 atoms) with many aromatic rings (10) and substantial branching (18) plus multiple rotatable bonds (10), consistent with a rigid-but-rotatable “molecular rotor” architecture where restriction of intramolecular motion in aggregates could enhance emission. The heteroatom content is low (3 total; one fused nsnc ring), and the donor/acceptor partition proxy is moderate (0.5), which weakens an a priori strong push–pull ICT assignment; however, the extended conjugation and multiple aryl–aryl/exocyclic double-bond linkages can still support some CT character depending on substituent polarization and twisting. Geometry proxies show high planarity_proxy (~0.97) and compactness_proxy (~0.95) for the selected conformer, suggesting a largely conjugated, relatively compact structure in that conformer; meanwhile torsion_candidate_count=10 and conformer_dispersion_proxy=1.0 indicate multiple torsional degrees of freedom and meaningful conformational variability, which keeps TICT-like dark-state formation plausible at specific aryl-aryl junctions under low-viscosity conditions. Overall, macro evidence favors a predominantly neutral-aromatic, conjugated chromophore with rotatable peripheral phenyls (supporting RIM/AIE plausibility) while not excluding ICT/TICT because torsional access and extended conjugation remain present.
Capability limit note: This macro step is limited to deterministic topology analysis and use of existing geometry proxies from the shared prepared structure context. It cannot compute S1 character/brightness, quantify ICT/TICT energetics, or model aggregation/packing; it can only flag structural features that make those mechanisms more or less plausible.

[focus_selection] Select the macro structural focus areas requested by the Planner instruction. [topology_analysis] Summarize rotor topology, ring systems, conjugation, and donor-acceptor layout. [geometry_proxy_analysis] Summarize planarity, torsion, compactness, and conformer-dispersion proxies.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier evidence on close-family polyarylethylene/TPE-like AIE systems supports locally-excited (LE)/neutral-aromatic emission with nonradiative decay dominated by intramolecular rotations (RIR/RIM), which aligns with this hetero-poor polyaryl scaffold; internal data did not surface strong CT/torsion-dark-state signatures.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier evidence indicates TICT is a known pathway in some rotor systems but is most strongly supported in donor–acceptor differentiated cases; for hetero-poor polyarylethylenes, literature emphasis is more on RIR/RIM-driven LE behavior than obligatory TICT. Still plausible given rotatable bonds and missing torsion-angle/CT observables internally.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Verifier precedent for strong ICT typically requires clearer donor–acceptor partitioning/solvatochromism; this molecule is hetero-poor with weak D–A differentiation and CT metrics were not recoverable internally.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty remains because internal discriminators (ct_localization_proxy, torsion geometry descriptor) are unavailable, leaving room for mixed/atypical behavior beyond the screened families.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: No proton-donor motif; ESIPT precondition not met.

raw_response.portfolio_screening_summary: Top working hypothesis is a neutral-aromatic/RIM-type AIE for a large polyaromatic hydrocarbon-rich scaffold. However, ICT and TICT remain credible because the molecule contains extended conjugation plus multiple rotatable/sterically congested linkages; we have not yet screened excited-state charge-transfer character, oscillator strength trends, or whether twisting creates dark states. First round will gather macro heuristics and one bounded baseline S0/S1 computation to reduce this coverage debt.
raw_response.screening_focus_summary: A single baseline S0 geometry + S1 vertical excitation (with charge-density/CT diagnostics if available) can quickly indicate whether S1 is locally-excited and bright (supporting neutral-aromatic/RIM) versus CT-like or dark/twist-associated (supporting ICT/TICT). Macro will provide structural/precedent cues to contextualize the computed result.
raw_response.diagnosis: Task: assess the likely AIE mechanism for the provided C58H40N2S polyaromatic molecule. Current top1 is neutral aromatic (RIM/RIR-driven AIE typical of bulky multi-phenyl conjugated scaffolds). Current top2 runner-up is ICT, with TICT also credible due to multiple rotatable bonds and potential steric twisting; ESIPT is effectively excluded by missing proton-transfer functionality. Shared prepared structure context is available (101 atoms, 10 conformers, selected conformer id 5, high planarity/compactness proxies).
